task: Visit NASA’s official website and locate the main page listing all upcoming launch events. For each scheduled event, clearly provide a direct link to its official NASA event page and explicitly indicate whether the mission is crewed.\nFor each crewed mission, if crew members have already been announced, clearly provide each crew member’s full name, nationality, and a link to an informational page about that person, such as a biography, profile, or general introduction. If crew members have not yet been announced, explicitly state this.
graderReasoning: The agent successfully completed the task by navigating to NASA's official website and locating the main page listing all upcoming launch events. It correctly extracted all 19 scheduled events from both pages of the launch schedule, providing a direct link to each official NASA event page. For each event, it explicitly indicated whether the mission was crewed. For crewed missions where crew members were announced (Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 and NASA's SpaceX Crew-13), the agent accurately provided each crew member’s full name, nationality, and a link to an informational page (NASA profile, CSA profile, or Wikipedia). For crewed missions where crew members had not yet been announced (Artemis III, IV, V), it explicitly stated this. The agent demonstrated good problem-solving by correcting initial tool usage errors and splitting websearch queries to adhere to limits. The final output is comprehensive, accurate, and well-formatted, meeting all user requirements.
